result:
The file is well-formed and complete. I've written `runs/pending_experiment.json` with a genuine full pivot:

**Design: Spatial mixture-of-experts** — a 64-way coarse macro-region classifier (from-scratch trunk) dispatches to one of 64 disjoint 16-way local fine-cell experts, each trained only on samples from its own region (teacher-forced loss routing).

Key reasoning:
- Memory shows the real bottleneck is **ranking**, not decode: true cell is top-1 only ~2% of the time despite being in the top-20 (of 1024) 33-58% of the time.
- All 14 prior experiments (including the just-completed exp49 dense-per-patch consensus, which trained and scored 2655m — much worse) kept one thing intact: a single shared softmax where every cell must stay comparable to every other cell, including distant lookalikes.
- This design removes that shared-scale constraint by splitting the decision into two disjoint stages, so no local expert ever has to out-rank a far-away lookalike.
- Backbone is fully replaced (from-scratch 4-stage conv trunk, no torchvision/mobilenet references, no `.pt` loading) to satisfy this round's ban.
- Every non-frozen stage is marked `changed: true`.
- Kept implementation risk low (single reshaped linear layer instead of a ModuleList, no C4 batch-stacking, teacher-forced training avoids fragile argmax-through-training-loss issues) since the last several ambitious pivots (43/44/46/47) crashed or gate-failed before ever training.

This is now ready for the implementation stage.

tool_input.command:
cat /FROZEN 2>/dev/null || find / -maxdepth 2 -iname "FROZEN*" 2>/dev/null
echo "---"
ls pipeline/ 2>/dev/null
echo "---"
cat autoresearch/library.md 2>/dev/null | head -150
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE id=49;" | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null | head -100
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE id=49;" > /tmp/exp49_arch.json; python3 -m json.tool /tmp/exp49_arch.json
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE id=49;" > /tmp/exp49_arch.json
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE id=49;" > runs/_tmp_exp49_arch.json && python3 -m json.tool runs/_tmp_exp49_arch.json

python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('runs/pending_experiment.json')); print('OK, keys:', list(d.keys())); print('stages:', len(d['architecture']['stages'])); [print(s['name'], s['changed']) for s in d['architecture']['stages']]"

node -e "const d=require('./runs/pending_experiment.json'); console.log('keys:', Object.keys(d)); console.log('stages:', d.architecture.stages.length); d.architecture.stages.forEach(s=>console.log(s.name, s.changed));"
